scenario: Helios Retailer aggregates 4000 orders per day in Camunda 8.8 through a FEEL output mapping on a service task. The mapping must concatenate a list of SKU codes for one order into a single string with no separator between codes, and must silently skip any null entries in the SKU code list rather than emit a null result.
question: Which FEEL call concatenates a list of strings into a single result while ignoring null items and using no separator?

options.text: string join(list, ", ") joins the strings into one result with a fixed comma between elements and skips null items in the list
concatenate(list) joins the strings into one result with no separator between elements and skips null items in the input list
string join(list, "") joins the strings into one result with no separator between elements and returns null when any item is null
string join(list) joins the strings into one result with no separator between elements and skips null items in the input list

a.text: Incorrect. The two-argument form inserts the delimiter between elements; the requirement is no separator, and that is satisfied only by the single-argument string join(list) form on a list of strings.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no concatenate(list) function in FEEL for joining a list of strings; the documented mechanism is string join with one or more arguments.

c.text: Incorrect. With an empty string delimiter the result has no separator, but string join skips null items rather than returning null when an item is null; only a non-string non-null item produces null.

d.text: Correct. string join(list) joins a list of strings into a single string with no separator between elements and silently ignores null items in the list, matching the SKU output requirement.

explanation: string join(list) joins a list of strings into a single string with no separator between elements. If an item is null it is ignored; if an item is neither a string nor null, the function returns null.
